advancedsteelmaking-text:
Advanced Steelmaking
One step for a seraph, a thousand plates for all of humanity.

Steelmaking Expanded adds a full industrial pipeline that works in two stages. Stage I stands up a blast furnace and its support machinery to smelt iron in bulk. Stage II bolts a Bessemer converter onto that line to refine the molten iron into steel. Build the iron stage first and get it running reliably — the steel stage is expensive and feeds directly off it.
Most of these structures need tier 3 refractory bricks, which means you need to reach the steel age before building them!

The chain is described in four companion articles:
- The Blast Furnace — the heart of the operation and its hoppers.
- Hot Blast & Gas Handling — cowper stoves, the smoke stack and the gas network.
- Molten Metal & Casting — canals, taps, molds, barrels and slag.
- The Bessemer Converter — stage II, blowing iron into steel.
The pipes, boilers and steam engines that power it all come from the companion mod — see Steam Power.

A production run, in short
1. Stock the reinforced hopper with crushed iron ore, crushed coke and lime, and enable dropping on the bell hopper.
2. Let the shaft fill with blast mix, ignite it, and close the door.
3. Push Air through a charged cowper stove into the tuyeres as hot blast (an air blower on a steam engine provides the Blast); vent spare exhaust through the smoke stack.
4. Watch the temperature climb past ~1482 °C into the Melting phase; alternate your two cowper stoves so the blast never goes cold.
5. When iron and slag have pooled, open the molten metal taps to drain them down the canals into molds or barrels — before anything solidifies.
6. Keep the hopper supplied, the exhaust flowing, and repeat.

Once the iron line runs smoothly, wire the converter into it and the same canals will carry steel.
handbook-blastfurnace-text:
The Blast Furnace
The blast furnace is the heart of the operation: a tall multiblock built mostly from refractory bricks, with an iron door, two tuyeres (air intakes) low on the shaft and two gas outlets high up. Place the door block and shift + rightmouse it to see the build outline, then fill in every highlighted block until the structure reports complete.

Blocks required:
- 98 x Refractory Bricks (tier 3)
- 1 x Blast Furnace Door (the control block, placed first)
- 2 x Tuyere
- 2 x Molten Metal Tap
- 2 x Gas Outlet
- 1 x Reinforced Hopper
- 1 x Bell Hopper
The hollow shaft inside is left empty — the hoppers fill it with blast mix.

Feeding it. The furnace does not eat ore directly; it burns blast mix, a packed pile of ore and fuel. You never craft blast mix by hand — it is produced by the pair of hoppers stacked above the shaft. The Reinforced Hopper is the magazine: open it (rightmouse) and load three things — crushed iron ore, crushed coke, and lime (flux). Crush iron ore and coke in a pulverizer; coke is baked from coal in a coke oven, and even scrap iron bits can be crushed back into crushed iron. The hopper combines 12 crushed iron + 3 crushed coke + 1 lime into 16 blast mix and stockpiles it internally.

Below it sits the Bell Hopper, which drips the stockpiled blast mix down into the shaft. Toggle dropping with ctrl + rightmouse on the reinforced hopper; it stops on its own once the furnace is full.

Nothing is wasted if you fumble a pile. Blast mix behaves just like a coal pile: rightmouse the ground to lay loose mix back down, stack more onto it, and feed it back to the furnace. It even burns as a normal fuel in a pinch.

Firing. Let the bell hopper fill the furnace, ignite the blast mix inside, and close the door. Once every pile in the hearth is burning, the door is shut and the exhaust outlets are not choked, the furnace begins firing and climbs toward ~1400 °C on its own. Hold it above iron's melting point (~1482 °C, see the hot blast article) for a few minutes and it enters the Melting phase, converting blast mix into a reservoir of molten iron and molten slag.

The fire is fragile. Opening the door, running out of blast mix, a choked or backfed exhaust, or letting the molten reservoir overflow will all snuff it out — and an extinguished melt solidifies in the hearth as iron blocks you must mine out. Keep the exhaust flowing and the door shut while it works.
handbook-hotblast-text:
Why hot blast? A raw blast furnace tops out around 1400 °C — not enough to melt iron. To push past that you reclaim the heat it throws away.

The gas network. Everything breathes through gas pipes (from Pipes and Power Expanded). Gas comes in three kinds: ordinary Air, hot Exhaust spilling from the furnace and converter, and forced Blast (pressurised air). A few special blocks shape the flow:
- The Cornish Air Blower — a steam-engine sub-machine — pressurises Air into Blast; this is what forces gas through the cowper stoves and into the tuyeres.
- Valves open and close a line by hand — essential for swapping the cowper stoves.
- Pressure valves open automatically above their gate pressure.
- Outlets connect the network to a structure's face (a tuyere, a stove, the converter intake).
Each network tracks its gas type, volume and temperature; an open-ended pipe leaks.

The Cowper Stove — a heat regenerator. Pipe the furnace's Exhaust into a Cowper Stove and it soaks that heat into its brick core ("charging"); burning a coal pile inside charges it faster, with anthracite fastest. Beware: burning low grades of coal makes the stove itself produce exhaust from its main output!
Once charged, route plain Blast through the same stove and it pours out scorching hot. Feed that into the furnace tuyeres and the internal temperature jumps toward ~1700 °C.

Blocks required (per stove — you want two):
- 48 x Refractory Bricks (tier 3)
- 1 x Cowper Stove Intake (the control block)
- 4 x Heat Sink
- 2 x Gas Outlet
- 1 x Gas Passthrough
- 1 x Iron Hatch Door
One interior slot is left open for the coal pile that charges the core.

A single stove only does one job at a time, and a charged core runs down as you draw blast from it. Run two stoves as a pair: while one charges on exhaust, the other discharges hot blast into the furnace; when the discharging one cools, swap their roles with valves. Keep exhaust and air on separate paths — if they mix inside a stove it cannot heat.

The Smoke Stack. A blast furnace produces more exhaust than the stoves can swallow. The Smoke Stack vents the surplus to the sky — a backed-up exhaust line chokes and extinguishes the furnace, so treat it as the safety valve of the whole system.
Blocks required:
- 1 x Smoke Stack Intake (the control block)
- 60 x Fire Clay Bricks
handbook-casting-text:
Molten Canals. Liquid metal is plumbed, not carried. Lay a Molten Canal (Start) as the anchor of a network, then run straights, bends and junctions from it — they are crafted from cobblestone and fire clay, and each canal keeps the look of the rock you built it from. Drain the furnace with its Molten Metal Taps — the lower tap pours iron, the upper tap pours slag — each into the start of a canal below it; toggle a tap with rightmouse. A Canal Tap at the far end pours the metal back out.

Sealing — a manual valve. rightmouse an empty straight canal with 4 fire clay and it caps over, severing the network at that block. Only a drained canal can be sealed. Reopen it with a chisel (recovers 2 fire clay).

Mind the temperature. Metal in the canals slowly cools; if it drops too far it solidifies in place — a hardened canal stops flowing and must be chipped clear (chisel + hammer once fully cold; you recover most of the metal as bits, and those bits crush back into crushed iron for the furnace). Keep runs short, keep them fed, and empty them before they or the furnace go cold.

Casting. A canal tap pours into two destinations:
- A Mold Pedestal casts small tool molds: shift + rightmouse places or removes the mold, ctrl + rightmouse toggles pouring. This mod adds larger casting molds — plate, quad rod and double ingot — that you clay-form and fire like any tool mold. The big anvil and helve hammer molds go under the canal tap itself instead.
- A Molten Barrel — bulk storage for liquid metal. Use it to buffer excess iron, or to hold slag from the top tap.

Handling hot molds. A freshly filled mold is dangerously hot: carrying it bare-handed burns you (wear heavy leather or blacksmith's gloves). While the metal is still liquid, the mold can only ride in your empty active hand — picking it up with anything else in hand, stashing it in a bag, or racking it spills the metal instantly. Set it down and let it harden, then collect the casting with a plain rightmouse.

Slag is not waste. Solidified slag grinds into powdered slag: mixed with slaked lime in a barrel it yields mortar, and spread on farmland it acts as a phosphate fertilizer — furnace byproduct into building stock and crop yield.
handbook-bessemer-text:
The Bessemer Converter takes molten iron and blows it into steel. It is the most demanding machine in the mod — it draws both mechanical power and a Blast gas supply, and only works as the final stage of an already-running molten line. Do not attempt it until your iron tier is established.

Building it. The converter is a 3x3x3 vessel raised through right-click construction. Place its control block first, then rightmouse it with 4 rusty gears and 12 iron or steel rods in your hotbar to start the vessel, and build up the construction stages until it reports complete. Around the vessel sit its service ports: a gas intake, a mechanical transmission, an input tap, and an output.

Blocks required:
- 1 x Bessemer Control (the control block, placed first)
- 1 x Bessemer Converter vessel — raised in place from 4 rusty gears + 12 iron/steel rods
- 1 x Bessemer Transmission
- 1 x Bessemer Gas Intake
- 1 x Molten Canal (Start)
- 2 x Molten Canal (Straight)
- 1 x Molten Canal (Tap)

Construction cost — raising the vessel. All stages together consume:
- 40 x Iron/Steel Plate
- 42 x Iron/Steel Nails & Strips
- 18 x Iron/Steel Rod
- 84 x Refractory Brick (tier 3)
- 84 x Fire Clay
- 3 x Gas Piping (Straight)

Plugging it in. Two services from the systems you already built:
- Mechanical power — connect an axle to the transmission so the vessel can tilt and blow.
- Blast — pipe Blast (from an air blower) into the gas intake. The intake must face the same way as the control block or it won't couple.
Then run a molten canal from a furnace tap into the converter's input tap, and a second canal off its output toward your molds.

Operating it.
1. Set the converter to Filling and pour molten iron in from the canal until charged.
2. Switch to Normal. With power and blast flowing, it refines the charge at ~1800 °C for about five minutes into steel.
3. Set it to Pouring to drain the finished steel into the output canal — from there it travels the molten network exactly like iron.

The same temperature rule applies inside the vessel: if the charge cools and solidifies before you pour it, break the converter with a steel pickaxe to recover the metal, then rebuild.
blockdesc-engineairblower-*: Steam-engine sub-machine that pressurises air into Blast for the furnace tuyeres and converter intake.
blockdesc-blastfurnacedoor*: The iron door of the blast furnace.
blockdesc-hopperreinforced*: Combines crushed ores into blast mix for the bell hopper below.
blockdesc-hopperbell*: Drips stockpiled blast mix down into the furnace shaft.
blockdesc-blastfurnace-tuyere*: A hot blast intake for the furnace shaft.
blockdesc-blastfurnacetap*: Drains the furnace's molten reservoir into a canal below.
blockdesc-slag*: A block of solidified slag from the blast furnace.
blockdesc-solidifiediron*: Iron that cooled and hardened before it could be tapped or poured.
blockdesc-cowperstove-intake*: The intake face of a cowper stove.
blockdesc-cowperstoveheatsink*: The core of the cowper stove that stores reclaimed exhaust heat.
blockdesc-smokestack-intake*: Vents surplus furnace exhaust to the sky.
blockdesc-moltencanal-start*: The anchor of a molten canal network.
blockdesc-moltencanal-bend*: A 90° bend in a molten canal run.
blockdesc-moltencanal-tap*: Pours metal out of the far end of a canal.
blockdesc-moltencanal-moldpedestal*: Holds a fired mold and fills it from the canal network.
blockdesc-moltenbarrel*: Bulk storage for liquid metal or slag.
blockdesc-convertercontrol*: Used to operate the bessemer converter.
blockdesc-converterbessemer*: Massive vessel that blows molten iron into steel.
blockdesc-convertertransmission*: Couples an axle's mechanical power to the converter so the vessel can tilt and blow.
itemdesc-slag*: A by-product tapped from the blast furnace.
itemdesc-powderedslag*: Ground slag, future mortar or fertilizer.
blockhelp-bessemer-spawn: Place converter vessel (needs 4 rusty gears + 12 iron/steel rods)
bessemer-err-incomplete-count: Structure is not complete. {0} blocks are missing or incorrect.
bessemer-err-notbuilt: The converter vessel is not finished being built.
bessemer-err-nopower: No mechanical power — connect an axle to the transmission.
bessemer-err-intake-misaligned: The gas intake must face the same way as the control block.
bessemer-info-intake-misaligned: Gas intake misaligned — it must face the same way as the control block.
bessemer-err-transmission-misaligned: The transmission must face the same way as the control block.
bessemer-info-transmission-misaligned: Transmission misaligned — it must face the same way as the control block.
bessemer-err-converter-present: The converter vessel is already in place.
bessemer-err-converter-blocked: The converter cannot be placed — clear the 3x3x3 space first.
bessemer-err-materials: Needs {0} rusty gears and {1} iron/steel rods in your hotbar.
bf-info-doorclosed: Door must be closed for firing!
bf-info-exhaustfull: Exhaust network is full! Production halted.
bf-info-needsmix: Blast Furnace needs more blast mix to fire!
bf-info-partiallylit: Piles are partially lit. Waiting for the fire to spread...
bf-info-ready: Blast Furnace is ready! Ignite the blast mix inside and close the door.
bf-error-incomplete: Structure is not complete. {0} blocks are missing or incorrect.
structure-incomplete-count: Structure is not complete. {0} blocks are missing or incorrect.
tap-err-nocanal: No canal start found below the tap!
bessemer-status-solidified: Solidified — break the converter to clear it
bessemer-status-filling-nometal: Filling — no metal in input tap
cowperstove-status-exhaustmix: Cannot heat up, exhaust mixes with blast!
game:ingameerror-smex-moltenspill: The molten metal spilled out of the mold!
game:ingameerror-smex-needemptyhands: The metal is still molten — take the mold with an empty hand.
game:ingameerror-smex-moldtoosmall: This mold is used with the mold pedestal.
game:ingameerror-smex-moldtoolarge: This mold will not fit on the pedestal.
game:ingameerror-smex-canaltoohot: Wait for the metal to harden!
game:ingameerror-smex-canalnotempty: Cannot seal a canal with molten metal!
